input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the bottom text input on Android. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` no longer works in .NET 10 (previously worked in .NET 9).

This is a known change in .NET MAUI / Android. In Android 11+ (API 30+), `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE` was deprecated, and in .NET MAUI 10 (targeting .NET 10), the recommended approach changed.

Let me provide a concise answer:

In .NET MAUI 10 / Android, the `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` was deprecated (it was deprecated in Android API 30). The modern replacement is to use **keyboard insets** via the `Edge-to-Edge` / `WindowInsets` API.


The best approach now is to leverage MAUI's built-in `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` feature, which automatically adjusts scrolling when the keyboard appears—this has been available since .NET 8 and handles the keyboard overlap issue without relying on the deprecated resize mode. If that doesn't fully solve it, I can look into manually handling `WindowInsets` for more granular control.
